sections.name: Ambush Copse
Ravages Of Revenge
sections.text: An endless wait for revenge invariably leaves an ambush copse riddled with mushrooms and termites. Apply the weak adjustment to such an ambush copse and give it weakness 10 to bludgeoning damage. During a period of dormancy, parts of the ambush corpse might break off, gain their own sentience, and make their way into a nearby camp or village. Use the stats for a twigjack to represent these stray pieces, whose arrival might presage the full awakening of an ambush copse.

Sawed and axe-hewn timber grind together as an ambush copse moves, smashing through the forest. Cuts and burns mar the bark of this gigantic, angry mass of whirling, deadly logs.  
  
 The forest never forgets. It remembers the hatchets, the rasping saws, and the smoke of crackling wood fires. It remembers the carts taking ancient trees away to cut, shape, and burn. Fey meddling or errant elemental magic draw such memories out alongside the rage and sorrow of the forest to form an ambush copse.   
  
An ambush copse exacts its ire on villages along the woodcutter's path or waits for intruders to step into its forest domain. While an ambush copse might be mistaken for a wounded arboreal, it can cease movement to appear as an overgrown pile of logs or a partially collapsed hovel. There, it waits for retribution.

traits.name: Elemental
traits.text: Elementals are creatures directly tied to an element and native to the elemental planes. Elementals don’t need to breathe.

traits.name: Plant
traits.text: Vegetable creatures have the plant trait. They are distinct from normal plants. Magical effects with this trait manipulate or conjure plants or plant matter in some way. Effects that manipulate plants have no effect in an area with no plants.

traits.name: Wood
traits.text:
Effects with the wood trait conjure or manipulate wood. Those that manipulate wood have no effect in an area without wood. Creatures with this trait consist primarily of wood or have a connection to magical wood.  
  
These planes consist of trees and other flora latticed in organic patterns. Wood planes are often perfectly constructed to match their desired purpose or environment but aren’t usually outright hostile to visitors.

automatic_abilities.name: Berserk
automatic_abilities.text: An ambush copse that sees fire or axes has a chance of going berserk. At the start of its turn, if it is aware of an axe or a fire the size of a lit torch or larger, the ambush copse must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or go berserk. A berserk ambush copse can't use concentrate actions and wildly attacks the nearest living creature, or the nearest object if no creatures are nearby.

automatic_abilities.name: Blinding Branches
automatic_abilities.trigger: A creature within 20 feet of the ambush copse leaves a square during a move action it's using

automatic_abilities.name: Requirements
automatic_abilities.effect: The ambush copse's elemental energy animates nearby tree branches to swat at the creature's face. The triggering creature must succeed at a DC 30 Reflex save or become blinded for 1 round.
automatic_abilities.text: The triggering creature is in forest terrain;

automatic_abilities.name: Felling Ambush
automatic_abilities.trigger: A creature moves within 10 feet of the ambush copse

automatic_abilities.name: Requirements
automatic_abilities.effect: The ambush copse makes a log Strike against the triggering creature. If the attack hits, the creature must attempt a DC 30 Reflex save or be knocked prone.
automatic_abilities.text: The ambush copse is disguised as trees or logs;

traits.name: Backswing
traits.text: You can use the momentum from a missed attack with this weapon to lead into your next attack. After missing with this weapon on your turn, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your next attack with this weapon before the end of your turn.

traits.name: Forceful
traits.text: This weapon becomes more dangerous as you build momentum. When you attack with it more than once on your turn, the second attack gains a circumstance bonus to damage equal to the number of weapon damage dice, and each later attack gains a circumstance bonus to damage equal to double the number of damage dice.

traits.name: Reach
traits.text: This weapon can be used to attack enemies up to 10 feet away instead of only adjacent enemies. For creatures with reach, the weapon increases their reach by 5 feet.

traits.name: Sweep
traits.text: This weapon makes wide swinging attacks. When you attack with this weapon, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your attack roll if you already attempted to attack a different target this turn using this weapon.

traits.name: Thrown
traits.text: You can throw this weapon as a ranged attack; it is a ranged weapon when thrown. You add your Strength modifier to damage as you would for a melee weapon. When this trait appears on a melee weapon, it also includes the range increment. Ranged weapons with this trait use the range increment in the weapon’s Range entry.

ability.name: Feign Copse

traits.name: Concentrate
traits.text: An action with this trait requires a degree of mental concentration and discipline.

ability.text: Until the next time it acts, the ambush copse appears to be a harmless patch of trees or logs. It has an automatic result of 43 (45 in forests) on Deception checks and DCs to pass as trees or logs.

ability.name: Pounding Smash
ability.text: Regardless of whether the Strike hits or misses, the ambush copse's melee Strikes create a 5-foot-square of difficult terrain in the target's space.

ability.name: Pulverizing Barrage
ability.text: The ambush copse makes three log Strikes, each at a –2 penalty, all targeting the same creature. The ambush copse's multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after it has made all three attacks. The ambush copse gains the clumsy 2 condition until the beginning of its next turn.

ability.name: Sunder Objects
ability.text: When an ambush copse damages an item or structure, it deals an additional 15 damage to that item or structure.
